mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing the present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean 20-day forward return in low-rate months is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0% signals excess labor market slack that reduces aggregate consumption demand and compresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return during UNRATE > 6.0% is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and retail sales indicates a broad demand collapse across both manufacturing and consumer sectors, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed the effect of either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce lower average 20-day returns than the simple average of the two independent single-variable signals over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in industrial production from a depressed level signals capacity normalization and inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations recover.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns following INDPRO rebounds occur with equal or greater frequency outside post-shock recovery regimes, or the Rank IC within the designated regime remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,100 thousand units signals accelerating construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns during HOUST > 1100 are not significantly higher than baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises while the federal funds rate is held below the inflation rate, deeply negative real interest rates compress real yields and stimulate rebalancing into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below inflation fails to generate a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns do not exceed 55% frequency.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, accelerating payroll employment growth signals expanding household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism weakens in late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns following PAYEMS accelerations are not significantly larger in early-cycle regimes than in late-cycle regimes over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2.0% signals acute supply-side disruption and margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as profitability expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines exceed 2.0% month-over-month, or the mean return is not significantly below zero.
